THOUGHT:
The current bibliography covers technical debt in ML systems (Sculley et al., 2015), adversarial/non-robust features (Ilyas et al., 2019), and robustness to common corruptions (Hendrycks & Dietterich, 2019). However, it lacks a foundational paper addressing the core statistical and optimization pitfall of *underspecification*, which directly explains why deep learning models frequently fail unpredictably in deployment despite high validation accuracy. Citing D'Amour et al. (2020) will fill gaps in Categories 2 (specific concepts), 5 (highlighting research gaps in evaluation metrics), and 7 (supporting arguments about training pipeline limitations). This paper is essential for framing the theoretical disconnect between empirical risk minimization and real-world reliability.
